description_long: This rock is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized boulder with a predominantly white and light gray coloration, featuring subtle hints of greenish and brownish tints in some crevices. Its surface is rough and uneven, with natural grooves, ridges, and a weathered texture that suggests exposure to the elements. The rock has a somewhat flattened top and a more angular, jagged base, making it appear stable when placed on the ground. Fine, net-like surface patterns are visible, possibly indicating mineral deposits or fossilized textures. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one side more protruding and the other more recessed, giving it a natural, uncut appearance. This type of rock would commonly be found as a landscaping or barrier element in urban parks or along sidewalks.
description: A rough, irregularly shaped white and gray rock with weathered grooves and a natural, asymmetrical form. Its surface is textured and slightly patterned, resembling a landscaping or barrier stone.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the rock, highlighting its textured surface and subtle color variations.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a more angular, jagged end of the rock, with deeper crevices and a compact profile.
description_view_2: This side displays the elongated length of the rock, emphasizing its uneven ridges and recessed areas.
description_view_3: This perspective shows a combination of the rock's rounded and angular features, with visible surface patterns and a slightly sloped top.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
